sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Grace Ross, is a 24-year-old female presenting with symptoms of discomfort, change in vaginal discharge, and odor in her vaginal region.
She reports that the right side of her vagina has become painful.
She denies any fevers or chills.
She recently learned that her partner has been engaging in sexual encounters with other people, and she wants to be evaluated for all STDs.
She reports that she has been experiencing a change in her vaginal discharge, which smells differently and is not the same color or consistency as usual.
She also reports that the right side of her vagina has been sore for an extended period.
She has been with her partner for three years, and he started cheating on her approximately three months ago.
She has had one abnormal Pap smear in the past, which was approximately six months ago, but it came back normal.
Past medical history:
The patient reports a history of an abnormal Pap smear at age 20, but it has not recurred since.
She has not had a Pap smear since then.
Review of systems:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The pelvic exam demonstrates no external lesions.
Normal labia majora and labia minora.
Normal physiological discharge with normal color and smell.
The cervix is normal.
The vaginal wall is normal.
No cervical motion tenderness and no adnexal tenderness or masses noted.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is at risk for STDs due to an abnormal Pap smear in the past and her partner's history of infidelity.
Problem list: Abnormal Pap smear past; High risk for STDs; Suspected STD infection.
Plan:
I discussed the need for STD testing with the patient, and I ordered tests for gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomoniasis, syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C.
I also ordered blood work for syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C.
The patient will be contacted if any abnormal results are found, and she will follow up with us in three days for results.
I also noted that the patient is high risk due to her abnormal Pap smear in the past and her partner's history of infidelity.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up in three days for test results.
